claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to ask you some questions about your child.

**First question: How old is your child?**

- Is your child under 16 years old?
- Is your child between 16-19 years old?
- Is your child 20 years old or older?

Please let me know your child's age, and if you have multiple children you're inquiring about, please tell me about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hi! Yes, I have two children I'd like to ask about. 

My eldest, Alex, is 12 years old, and my youngest, Blake, is 8. They both live at home with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me assess the eligibility for both Alex (12 years old) and Blake (8 years old).

Since both children are under 16, let me check the next requirements for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now, the next question:

**Are Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Given they're 12 and 8 years old, I would expect they're not in paid employment, but I need to confirm this to continue the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh no, they're not working at all! They're just kids - they're still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That's what I expected. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This would include benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in their own names. Given their ages (12 and 8), they're likely not receiving these benefits, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they don't receive any benefits in their own names. They're just children - I'm the one who handles all that sort of thing for the family.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, thank you for confirming. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake in any type of apprenticeship?**

Since they're 12 and 8 years old and you mentioned they're in school, I expect the answer is no, but I need to confirm they're not in any apprenticeship programs that might disqualify them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, not at all. They're way too young for that - they're just in regular school doing their lessons like normal kids their age.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That's exactly what I expected. Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check about **your residency status** as the claimant.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You mentioned you live in the UK with your children. To qualify, you need to meet at least ONE of these criteria:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You usually live in the UK as your main home
2. **Have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Have pre-settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme AND meet additional conditions (working, studying, sufficient resources, etc.)
4. **Living abroad as a Crown servant** or in certain qualifying countries

Since you mentioned you live in the UK, do you normally live in the UK as your main home?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - I normally live in the UK. This is my main home, and the children live here with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now I need to confirm your responsibility for Alex and Blake.

**Are you responsible for Alex and Blake?**

You qualify as responsible if you meet at least ONE of these criteria:

1. **Living with the children** - The children live with you
2. **Contributing to their upkeep** - You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care, including food, clothes, housing, etc.
3. **Paying maintenance** under a court order or agreement

Since you've already mentioned that Alex and Blake live at home with you, it sounds like you meet the first criterion. Can you confirm that they live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely - both Alex and Blake live with me. They're at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask an important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?**

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- A former partner
- Another family member

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child at a time. If someone else is claiming, you would need to agree on who should be the claimant before proceeding.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them and I haven't claimed it yet.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about where Alex and Blake are living:

**Are either Alex or Blake currently:**
- In hospital?
- In local authority care?
- Being fostered?
- Or are they living normally at home with you?

Based on what you've told me so far, it sounds like they're living normally at home with you, but I need to confirm there are no special circumstances.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're just living normally at home with me. Neither of them is in hospital, in care, or fostered - they're just regular kids living at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Almost there! Just one final question:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex and Blake's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is the council providing any financial support specifically for their housing or care costs? Given they live at home with you and you're responsible for them, I expect the answer is that the council is NOT paying, but I need to confirm this.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - the council isn't paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. I'm the one providing for them and covering all their costs myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Let me finalize the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

**Result: ELIGIBLE for both Alex and Blake**

You meet all the Child Benefit eligibility requirements for both of your children:

### For Alex (age 12):
✅ Under 16 years old (automatic qualification)
✅ Not in paid employment
✅ Not receiving benefits in their own right
✅ Not in an apprenticeship
✅ Lives with you in the UK
✅ No other person claiming for Alex
✅ Living normally at home
✅ Local council not paying for their care

### For Blake (age 8):
✅ Under 16 years old (automatic qualification)
✅ Not in paid employment
✅ Not receiving benefits in their own right
✅ Not in an apprenticeship
✅ Lives with you in the UK
✅ No other person claiming for Blake
✅ Living normally at home
✅ Local council not paying for their care

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and both children
3. **Provide birth certificates** (or adoption certificates) for Alex and Blake
4. **Be aware**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge (but you can still claim)
5. **Payments**: Child Benefit is typically paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### More Information:
You can find the claim form and more details here: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Congratulations, and I hope this helps your family!

reasonings: Alex (age 12) meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic qualification for age; (2) Not working (still in school); (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in any apprenticeship program; (5) Lives with the claimant in the UK; (6) Claimant has responsibility for Alex (living together); (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Living normally at home; (9) Local council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic qualification for age; (2) Not working (still in school); (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in any apprenticeship program; (5) Lives with the claimant in the UK; (6) Claimant has responsibility for Blake (living together); (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Blake; (8) Living normally at home; (9) Local council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children, Alex (12) and Blake (8). Both children meet all qualifying criteria as they are under 16, living at home with the claimant in the UK, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in apprenticeships, with no other claimants, and the local council is not paying for their care. The claimant meets UK residency requirements as they normally live in the UK and has responsibility for both children as they live together.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (age 12) meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic qualification for age; (2) Not working (still in school); (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in any apprenticeship program; (5) Lives with the claimant in the UK; (6) Claimant has responsibility for Alex (living together); (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Living normally at home; (9) Local council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic qualification for age; (2) Not working (still in school); (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right; (4) Not in any apprenticeship program; (5) Lives with the claimant in the UK; (6) Claimant has responsibility for Blake (living together); (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Blake; (8) Living normally at home; (9) Local council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children, Alex (12) and Blake (8). Both children meet all qualifying criteria as they are under 16, living at home with the claimant in the UK, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in apprenticeships, with no other claimants, and the local council is not paying for their care. The claimant meets UK residency requirements as they normally live in the UK and has responsibility for both children as they live together.
